body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"acceptance criteria define a concrete, non-overlapping scope distinct from the sibling 'part 1' child: This 'part 2 of 2' child is a placeholder stub from a deterministic fallback splitter, not a real decomposition. Parent ddx-74452926's own event log states: 'deterministic fallback split after agent decomposer returned invalid output' with ac_map entries 'Implement the first bounded slice' / 'Implement the remaining bounded slice' — neither maps to any of the parent's actual 10 numbered acceptance criteria (caller narrowing, Backend composite, internal/storage+internal/lifecycle relocation, factory.go, boundary test, lint cleanup, go test, pre-commit). Sibling ddx-3452fd6d ('part 1') is byte-identical to this bead except 'first' vs 'remaining', confirming no real scope boundary was drawn between the two children. A prior execution attempt on the sibling (attempt 20260706T022008-54379d63, codex/gpt-5.4-mini) returned no_changes citing 'the bounded try-command narrowing in this bead' — a scope description that matches neither child's actual content, showing the executing agent had to invent its own interpretation because none was given. Implementing this bead as written risks either duplicating the sibling's work or leaving gaps in the parent's real AC set (e.g. the compile-time assertion, boundary test, and lint-analyzer removal are not clearly assigned to either child).","fingerprint":"bc1444ffe32ab0e839dd87519eeb3754a05b3a02541442bc9761cc481b891668","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"bc1444ffe32ab0e839dd87519eeb3754a05b3a02541442bc9761cc481b891668"}
